item.title: Artificial micro-earthquake in Ticino: historic experiment in San Gottardo
item.description: Swiss, Italian and German researchers trigger a controlled earthquake in the San Gottardo massif to study the predictability of seismic events
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Swiss, Italian, and German researchers trigger artificial earthquake in the San Gottardo
- Experiment conducted in the BedrettoLab at 1,500 meters depth
- Microseisms recorded by hundreds of hypersensitive sensors

## Key facts
- **What**: Triggering of an artificial earthquake
- When: April 29, 2026
- Where: San Gottardo Massif, BedrettoLab
- Who: University of Aachen, ETH Zurich, INGV
- Depth: 1,500 meters

A group of Swiss, Italian, and German researchers successfully triggered an artificial earthquake in the San Gottardo massif for the first time, opening new perspectives for understanding and future earthquake predictability. The experiment went smoothly and was announced on Wednesday by the University of Aachen in Germany, which participates in the project along with ETH Zurich and Italy's National Institute of Geophysics and Volcanology. For several days, researchers injected high-pressure water into a fault zone near the BedrettoLab. This underground laboratory in Val Bedretto is located 1,500 meters deep in an abandoned tunnel and was built in the 1970s as a construction shaft for the Furka railway tunnel.

The goal was to controllably modify the tension between the rocks and thus cause microseisms. The experiment was a complete success. An entire series of small tremors was recorded. These extremely minor earthquakes were not felt on the surface. Hundreds of hypersensitive sensors were placed near the faults, the rock fractures along which earthquakes can occur. These devices were so reactive that they even recorded the earthquake that occurred in Japan on April 20, over 10,000 kilometers away.

### Objectives and results

Thanks to this experiment, the research group was able to measure an earthquake at its origin and no longer on the surface, as is usually the case. 'The aim,' explained Domenico Giardini, a seismologist, to RSI, 'is to go underground and see how it works when the earth decides to move. Usually, we learn something when a major earthquake occurs and causes immense damage, but we are never there, we are generally too far away, elsewhere or anyway on the surface. The earthquake, however, occurs at depth.' The signals obtained are 'incredible,' declared Florian Amann of the University of Aachen. Now we have unique data on the physics of earthquakes.

The project, launched seven years ago and named 'FEAR,' an acronym for Fault Activation and Earthquake Rupture, aims to improve earthquake predictability. Scientists want to better understand what happens before an earthquake occurs. A major earthquake is preceded by thousands of small tremors, the evolution of which contains a lot of information, observed Amann. So far, no reliable precursor signal of an earthquake has been identified, despite decades of research.

### Implications for seismic research

Since we cannot wait for an earthquake to occur in the right place, we trigger it directly, explained Stefan Wiemer of ETH Zurich to the Keystone-ATS agency before the experiment. These microseisms were followed live by three different teams. 'There are so many people involved that we were extremely tense, and the success was so extraordinary that now we first have to sit down,' said Amann, quoted in the press release. Currently, the water injected at high pressure into the fault is draining from the mountain. The current phase of the experiment ends here. In the coming weeks and months, new experiments will be conducted. It will also be necessary to analyze the enormous amounts of data collected. ### Practical Analysis: Implications for Seismic Research

The experiment conducted in the BedrettoLab represents a significant step towards understanding earthquakes and their predictability. The ability to trigger and monitor artificial earthquakes in a controlled environment offers scientists the opportunity to study phenomena that, under natural conditions, are difficult to observe. This type of research is fundamental to developing more accurate models and prediction tools that can help mitigate the risks associated with earthquakes.

### Comparison with the Previous Situation

Before this experiment, most of the information about earthquakes came from the analysis of natural events, often catastrophic. This approach had significant limitations, as the data were often fragmented and collected in emergency conditions. The experiment in the BedrettoLab allowed data to be collected systematically and in a controlled manner, offering a more comprehensive view of seismic dynamics. This change represents a crucial step towards a better understanding of earthquakes and a greater ability to predict their occurrence.

### Future Scenarios

In the coming months, researchers will continue to analyze the data collected during the experiment. This work could lead to new discoveries about the physics of earthquakes and improve the ability to predict seismic events. Furthermore, the results of this experiment could be used to develop new technologies and strategies for mitigating seismic risk, offering greater security to communities living in high-risk areas.

### Impact on the Scientific Community

The experiment in the BedrettoLab has attracted the attention of the international scientific community. The ability to trigger artificial earthquakes in a controlled environment represents a new frontier in seismic research. This type of research could open new possibilities for the study of other geological phenomena and contribute to a better understanding of the processes that govern the Earth.

### Conclusion

The experiment conducted in the BedrettoLab represents an important step forward in seismic research. The ability to trigger and monitor artificial earthquakes offers new opportunities to understand seismic dynamics and develop more accurate prediction tools. This work is fundamental to mitigating the risks associated with earthquakes and protecting communities living in high-risk areas.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### Concrete actions for researchers and institutions

For the researchers involved in the project, the coming weeks and months will be dedicated to analyzing the data collected during the experiment. This work will require a methodical and collaborative approach, involving experts from various disciplines. The institutions involved, such as RWTH Aachen University, ETH Zurich, and INGV, will need to coordinate their efforts to maximize the results of the research.

### Data analysis procedure

1. **Data collection**: Researchers will begin by collecting and organizing the data gathered during the experiment. This includes recording seismic shocks, measuring pressures and temperatures, and analyzing the images and videos recorded.
2. Preliminary analysis: The data will be analyzed to identify patterns and anomalies. This step is crucial for understanding seismic dynamics and identifying the factors that influence the triggering of earthquakes.
3. Modeling: The collected data will be used to develop mathematical models and simulations that can predict the behavior of earthquakes under different conditions.
4. Sharing results: The results of the analysis will be shared with the scientific community through publications, conferences, and workshops. This will help disseminate knowledge and promote further research in the field of seismology.

### Tools and resources

To support data analysis, researchers will use a variety of tools and resources, including data analysis software, advanced mathematical models, and visualization technologies. Additionally, they will collaborate with other research institutes and universities to access additional resources and specialized expertise.

### Conclusion and CTA

The experiment conducted in the BedrettoLab represents an important step forward in seismic research. The ability to trigger and monitor artificial earthquakes offers new opportunities to understand seismic dynamics and develop more accurate prediction tools. To further explore the implications of this experiment, consult our [seismic calculator](nav:calculator) and discover how research can contribute to greater safety for communities living in high-risk areas.

*Source: [tvsvizzera.it](https://www.tvsvizzera.it/tvs/relazioni-italo-svizzere/microterremoto-artificiale-in-ticino/91335322)*

item.title: ECB holds rates but inflation risks rise
item.description: The ECB keeps rates unchanged but warns of inflation risks due to Middle East conflict. Impacts for cross-border workers in Ticino.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- ECB rates unchanged: deposits 2%, refinancing 2.15%, marginal 2.40%
- Inflation risks increased due to Middle East conflict
- Concern over energy prices and confidence climate

## Key facts
- **What**: ECB decision on interest rates
- When: 30 April 2026
- Where: Eurotower, Frankfurt
- Who: ECB Governing Council
- Amount: Deposit rates 2%, refinancing 2.15%, marginal 2.40%
- Risk: Increased inflation due to Middle East conflict

The ECB has decided to keep the three key interest rates unchanged: the deposit rate at the central bank, the main refinancing operations rate, and the marginal lending rate remain at 2%, 2.15%, and 2.40%, respectively. However, in the final note of the Council, the Eurotower emphasizes that 'the upside risks to inflation and the downside risks to growth have intensified.'

The conflict in the Middle East has caused a sharp increase in energy prices, pushing inflation higher and weighing on the confidence climate. The implications of the war for medium-term inflation and economic activity will depend on the intensity and duration of the energy price shock as well as the extent of its indirect and second-round effects. The longer the war continues and the longer energy prices remain high, the greater the likely impact on broader measures of inflation and the economy.

### Impacts for cross-border workers

For cross-border workers who work in Ticino and reside in Italy, the rise in energy prices could have a significant impact on purchasing power. With inflation rising, the cost of living could increase, directly affecting household budgets. Moreover, the uncertain economic situation could influence investment and savings decisions.

The Governing Council explains that it is 'still in a favorable position to address the current uncertainty. The euro area has entered this period of high energy prices with inflation around the 2% target and an economy that has shown good resilience in recent quarters. Longer-term inflation expectations remain firmly anchored, although those over shorter horizons have increased significantly.'

To define the appropriate monetary policy stance, the Governing Council reiterates its intention to 'closely monitor the situation and adopt a data-dependent approach under which decisions are taken on a meeting-by-meeting basis.' 'In particular, decisions on interest rates will be based on its assessment of inflation prospects and the risks surrounding them, considering the new economic and financial data, as well as the dynamics of underlying inflation and the intensity of the transmission of monetary policy, without committing to a particular path for rates.' - ## Practical Analysis

The ECB's decision to keep rates unchanged, despite increased inflation risks, could have several implications for cross-border workers in Ticino. With rising energy prices, the cost of living could increase, directly affecting purchasing power. This could lead to a reduction in savings and greater attention to family budget management.

### Comparison with the previous situation

Before the conflict in the Middle East, inflation in the euro area was around the 2% target, and the economy had shown good resilience. However, with the rise in energy prices, inflation could exceed this target, negatively affecting economic growth. For cross-border workers, this could mean greater uncertainty about the future and a need to adapt to the new economic conditions.

### Possible Scenarios

If the conflict in the Middle East were to persist, energy prices could remain high for an extended period. This could lead to sustained inflation, with consequent negative impacts on the economy and the purchasing power of cross-border workers. In this scenario, it might be necessary to adopt more rigorous savings and budget management measures.

On the other hand, if the conflict were to be resolved quickly, energy prices could return to lower levels, reducing the impact on inflation and the economy. In this case, cross-border workers could benefit from greater economic stability and an improvement in purchasing power.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## Concrete actions

To address the current economic situation, cross-border workers can adopt various strategies. First and foremost, it is important to carefully monitor the evolution of energy prices and inflation. This can help make informed decisions about budget management and investments.

### Concrete procedures

1. **Monitor energy prices**: Keeping track of energy prices and other living costs can help identify trends and adapt the budget accordingly.
2. Reduce energy consumption: Adopting measures to reduce energy consumption can help contain costs and mitigate the impact of rising prices.
3. Diversify investments: Diversifying investments can help reduce risk and protect capital in an uncertain economic context.
4. Use calculation tools: Using tools such as the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) can help plan the budget and manage finances more effectively.

### Deadlines and required documents

There are no immediate deadlines related to the ECB's decision, but it is important to stay informed about future meetings of the Governing Council and the decisions that may be made. Additionally, it is advisable to have documents such as the employment contract, energy bills, and consumption data readily available for more effective budget management.

### Useful tools

For more effective financial management, cross-border workers can use various tools available on the site. For example, the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) can help plan the budget and manage finances more effectively. Additionally, the [insurance comparator](nav:health) can help find the best options for health coverage, while the [pension calculator](nav:pension) can help plan for the future.

*Source: [varesenoi.it](https://www.varesenoi.it/2026/04/30/leggi-notizia/articolo/bce-mantiene-tassi-fermi-ma-rischi-inflazione-aumentati.html)*

item.title: ICT Ticino: 18,000 workers seek recognition
item.description: Ticino's ICT sector, with 18,000 employees, asks for representation in the Tripartite Commission. The State Council acknowledges its strategic importance.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- ICT sector with 18,000 workers in Ticino
- Request to join the Tripartite Commission
- State Council recognizes strategic importance

## Key facts
- **What**: Request for representation in the Tripartite Commission
- When: April 29, 2026
- Where: Bellinzona
- Who: ATED, TECH, ICT and Digital Ticino umbrella association
- Amount: 14,000-18,000 employees in the ICT sector

The ICT and digital sector in Ticino, increasingly central to economic development, remains underrepresented in institutional mechanisms related to the labor market. ATED, the TECH, ICT and Digital Ticino umbrella association, has raised this issue and initiated direct discussions with the State Council. In its official response, the State Council recognized the strategic role of digital transformation and ICT technologies for the development of the canton, emphasizing their impact on competitiveness, innovation, and service quality.

Regarding ATED's request to join the Tripartite Commission, the State Council noted that nominations 'follow the current legal framework and come from employer representative organizations.' Nevertheless, the association was invited to the commission's meeting on April 29, 2026, where it presented the Ticino ICT sector. President Luca Mauriello and Director Cristina Boggia Giotto reiterated a key point: 'ICT is now a cross-cutting infrastructure of the economy, but it is not adequately represented in the places where working conditions are defined.'

### The complexity of the ICT sector

The ICT sector in Ticino comprises over 200 distinct roles, often reduced to a simplified view. This perspective risks leading to inadequate regulatory tools. Among the critical issues highlighted is the current Standard Work Contract (CNC) for IT, considered not aligned with the sector's real value. According to the association, 'inadequate minimum wages can lead to effects such as dumping, talent flight, reduced quality, and weakened competitiveness.' ## Analysis of Implications
The request by ATED to be represented in the Tripartite Commission is a crucial step for the ICT sector in Ticino. The Tripartite Commission is a body that brings together representatives of employers, workers, and the government, playing a key role in defining working conditions and economic policies. ATED's presence could lead to better representation of the specific needs of the ICT sector, which is growing rapidly and increasingly central to the Ticino economy.

### Comparison with the Previous Situation
Before ATED's request, the ICT sector in Ticino had no formal representation in the Tripartite Commission. This led to several challenges, including the lack of adequate regulatory tools and difficulty in attracting and retaining qualified talent. ATED's presence could help overcome these challenges, ensuring the sector's voices are heard and policies are aligned with the market's real needs.

### Future Scenarios
If ATED's request is accepted, several positive changes for the ICT sector in Ticino could be observed. These include the revision of the Normal Work Contract (CNL) for informatics, the introduction of more favorable policies for attracting talent, and the creation of a more competitive work environment. However, the process of reviewing regulations and policies could be long and complex, requiring the involvement of all stakeholders.

### Comparative Table: Current vs. Future Situation
| Aspect | Current Situation | Future Situation (if request is accepted) |
|---------|------------------|-----------------------------------------------------|
| Representation in the Tripartite Commission | Absent | Present |
| Alignment of CNL with the sector's real value | Inadequate | Adequate |
| Attraction of Talent | Difficult | Facilitated |
| Sector Competitiveness | Weak | Strengthened |

### Procedure for CNL Revision
1. **Request Presentation**: ATED presents the CNL revision request to the Tripartite Commission.
2. Needs Analysis: The Tripartite Commission analyzes the ICT sector's needs and compares them with existing regulations.
3. Stakeholder Consultation: The Tripartite Commission consults representatives of employers, workers, and the government to gather feedback and suggestions.
4. Regulatory Revision: The Tripartite Commission proposes CNL changes to align it with the ICT sector's needs.
5. Approval and Implementation of Changes: Proposed changes are approved by stakeholders and implemented.

### Implications for Cross-Border Workers
Cross-border workers in the ICT sector in Ticino could benefit from better representation and more favorable working conditions. The CNL revision could lead to more adequate minimum wages and increased sector competitiveness, improving career opportunities and quality of life for workers.

### Useful Tools
To delve into the practical implications for cross-border workers, the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) can be used to compare current salaries with those anticipated under new regulations. Additionally, the [permit comparator](nav:permit-compare) can help understand differences between various work permit types and their conditions.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## Concrete actions for ICT workers

For ICT sector workers in Ticino, there are several concrete actions that can be taken to benefit from potential regulatory changes. First and foremost, it's important to stay up-to-date with the latest news and developments in the sector. ATED regularly provides updates and information through its website and social media channels.

### Procedure for requesting a revision of the CNL

1. **Feedback collection**: ICT sector workers can provide feedback and suggestions to ATED regarding their experiences and specific needs.
2. Participation in consultations: ATED organizes consultations with workers to gather feedback and suggestions.
3. Submission of proposals: ATED submits CNL revision proposals to the Tripartite Commission.
4. Progress monitoring: Workers can monitor the progress of the revision proposals through ATED's website and social media channels.
5. Implementation of changes: Once approved, the changes to the CNL are implemented, and workers can benefit from the new working conditions.

### Useful tools for workers

To delve deeper into the practical implications for workers, you can use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to compare current salaries with those predicted by the new regulations. Additionally, the [permit comparator](nav:permit-compare) can help understand the differences between the various types of work permits and their respective conditions.

### Final CTA

For more information and to stay up-to-date with the latest news and developments in the ICT sector in Ticino, visit the ATED website at [ATED](https://www.ated.ch) and follow their social media channels. Additionally, use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to compare current salaries with those predicted by the new regulations and the [permit comparator](nav:permit-compare) to understand the differences between the various types of work permits and their respective conditions.

*Source: [tio.ch](https://www.tio.ch/ticino/attualita/1922137/un-settore-da-18mila-lavoratori-in-cerca-di-riconoscimento-davanti-alle-istituzioni)*

item.title: Mobile and internet costs rise from 2026
item.description: Sunrise increases mobile and internet rates starting August 2026. Here's what changes for cross-border workers in Ticino.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Sunrise raises tariffs from August 2026
- Increases up to 2 francs per contract
- Yallo and Lebara also affected

## Key facts
- **What**: Sunrise, Yallo, Lebara tariff increases
- When: From August 2026
- Where: Switzerland, Canton Ticino
- Who: Sunrise, Yallo, Lebara
- Amount: +1.50 CHF for main contract, +0.75 CHF for multiple subscriptions, +0.50-2 CHF for Yallo and Lebara

Sunrise has announced that starting from August 2026, it will increase the prices of all mobile telephony and Internet subscriptions. The planned increases are 1.50 francs for each main contract of private and business customers and 75 cents for multiple or combined subscriptions. For Yallo and Lebara brand subscriptions, the increases will range from 50 cents to 2 francs per month, depending on the type of contract. The price adjustments will apply to both existing subscriptions and those of new customers.

The increases do not affect specific offers for commercial customers and custom B2B solutions, as well as offers from secondary brands Swype and CHmobile, Sunrise Prepaid and Home Security, additional services (options), and installment payments for devices (payment plans).

### Communication to users
Private and business customers affected will be informed in writing, via the bill, starting from June 1, 2026. Sunrise is not the only one taking this path: Swisscom raised prices on April 1, 2026, while Salt will do the same with the prices of some mobile telephony subscriptions starting from June 1, 2026.

### Impact on cross-border workers
For cross-border workers residing in Ticino and working in Italy, these increases could affect the monthly budget allocated to communications. It is important to consider these variations in the calculation of the cost of living and in financial planning. Furthermore, with the increase in tariffs, it may be useful to evaluate alternative offers or combined packages that offer a better quality-price ratio.

### Comparison with other companies
While Sunrise has announced increases starting from August 2026, other companies such as Swisscom and Salt have already started to review their tariffs. This trend could indicate a general tendency in the Swiss telecommunications market, with possible repercussions on all users, including cross-border workers.

### What to do
If you are a cross-border worker using Sunrise, Yallo, or Lebara services, it is advisable to monitor official communications and evaluate the available options. It may be useful to compare offers from different companies to find the most convenient solution. Furthermore, you can use tools such as the [monthly expense calculator](nav:cost-of-living) to have a clear view of expenses and plan accordingly. ### Analysis of Implications
The increase in tariffs for mobile phone and internet services could have a significant impact on cross-border workers living in Ticino and working in Italy. These increases could affect the monthly budget dedicated to communications, especially for those using Sunrise, Yallo, or Lebara services.

### Comparison with Previous Tariffs
Before these increases, Sunrise tariffs were considered competitive in the Swiss market. However, with the price increase, it may be useful to evaluate other available options. For example, comparing Swisscom and Salt offers could reveal more convenient solutions, especially for those who frequently use mobile phone and internet services.

### Possible Scenarios
One possible scenario is that cross-border workers decide to change operators to find more advantageous tariffs. This could lead to increased competition in the Swiss telecommunications market, with potential benefits for consumers. However, it is essential to consider the quality of service and network coverage, in addition to the price.

### Financial Planning
With the increase in tariffs, it is crucial for cross-border workers to review their financial planning. Using tools like the [monthly expense calculator](nav:cost-of-living) can help manage the budget better and identify potential savings. Additionally, it may be useful to evaluate combined packages that offer integrated services at a reduced cost.

### Comparison with Other Regions
It is interesting to note that the increase in tariffs in Switzerland could be influenced by external factors, such as tax policies and European regulations. For example, the new tax agreement between Italy and Switzerland could have an indirect impact on telecommunications tariffs, affecting the competitiveness of the market.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### Concrete actions
For cross-border workers who want to manage the increase in rates as best as possible, it is important to follow some concrete steps. First of all, it is essential to monitor the official communications from Sunrise, Yallo and Lebara to be informed about the increases and any promotional offers.

### Steps to follow
1. **Evaluate the available options**: Compare the offers of different telecommunications companies to find the most convenient solution.
2. Use calculation tools: Use the [monthly expense calculator](nav:cost-of-living) to have a clear view of the expenses and plan accordingly.
3. Consider combined packages: Evaluate packages that offer integrated services at a reduced cost, such as telephony, Internet and TV.
4. Monitor official communications: Receive official communications via invoice and evaluate the available options.
5. Plan the budget: Review the financial planning to adapt to the rate increases.

### Useful tools
To help cross-border workers manage the increase in rates as best as possible, various tools available on the site can be used. For example, the [monthly expense calculator](nav:cost-of-living) can help identify potential savings and plan the budget. Furthermore, the [offer comparator](nav:exchange) can be useful for comparing the rates of different companies and finding the most convenient solution.

### Conclusion
The increase in rates for mobile telephony and Internet could have a significant impact on cross-border workers living in Ticino and working in Italy. However, with careful planning and the use of adequate tools, it is possible to manage this situation as best as possible and find convenient solutions. Don't forget to use the [monthly expense calculator](nav:cost-of-living) to have a clear view of your expenses and plan accordingly.

*Source: [laregione.ch](https://www.laregione.ch/svizzera/svizzera/1922037/telefonare-e-navigare-costera-un-po-di-piu)*

item.title: ECB: rates unchanged, impact on Ticino
item.description: The ECB keeps interest rates unchanged, with implications for cross-border workers and the Ticino economy.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- The ECB keeps interest rates unchanged.
- Deposit rate at 2.00%, refinancing at 2.15%, marginal at 2.40%.
- Inflation and growth under observation.

## Key facts
- **What**: ECB decision on interest rates.
- When: 30 April 2026.
- Where: Frankfurt, Germany.
- Who: Governing Council of the European Central Bank.
- Amount: Deposit rate at 2.00%, refinancing at 2.15%, marginal at 2.40%.

The European Central Bank has decided to keep the three key interest rates unchanged. In detail, the interest rates on deposits with the central bank, on main refinancing operations and on marginal lending operations will remain unchanged at 2.00%, 2.15% and 2.40%, respectively. The decision was taken in light of new information that is broadly in line with the previous assessment of inflation prospects. However, the upside risks to inflation and the downside risks to growth have intensified.

The conflict in the Middle East has caused a sharp increase in energy prices, pushing up inflation and weighing on the confidence climate. The implications of the war for medium-term inflation and economic activity will depend on the intensity and duration of the energy price shock as well as the extent of its indirect and second-round effects. The longer the war continues and the longer energy prices remain high, the greater the likely impact on broader measures of inflation and the economy.

### Impact on Canton Ticino

For cross-border workers who work in Switzerland and reside in Italy, the ECB's decision to keep rates unchanged could have significant implications. High inflation and energy prices could affect purchasing power and economic stability. Furthermore, the situation could influence investment returns and credit costs, crucial elements for those operating across the Italian-Swiss border.

The Governing Council of the ECB is still in a favorable position to address the current uncertainty. The euro area has entered this period of high energy prices with inflation around the 2% target and an economy that has shown good resilience in recent quarters. Longer-term inflation expectations remain firmly anchored, although those over shorter horizons have increased significantly.

To define the appropriate monetary policy stance, the Governing Council will closely monitor the situation and adopt a data-dependent approach under which decisions are taken on a meeting-by-meeting basis. In particular, decisions on interest rates will be based on its assessment of inflation prospects and the risks surrounding them, considering the new economic and financial data, as well as the dynamics of underlying inflation and the intensity of the transmission of monetary policy, without committing to a particular path for rates. ### Analysis of the implications for cross-border workers

The ECB's decision to keep interest rates unchanged has several implications for cross-border workers who work in Switzerland and reside in Italy. Firstly, high inflation could erode purchasing power, making it harder for cross-border workers to manage daily expenses. Additionally, high energy prices could affect transportation and heating costs, crucial elements for those who commute daily between the two countries.

### Comparison with the previous situation

Before the ECB's decision, interest rates had already been increased to combat inflation. However, the current situation is more complex due to the conflict in the Middle East, which has led to a sharp rise in energy prices. This scenario requires a more cautious approach from the ECB, which must balance the need to control inflation with that of supporting economic growth.

### Possible scenarios

If inflation continues to rise, the ECB may be forced to review its monetary policy and raise interest rates. This could have a negative impact on credit costs and investment returns, affecting cross-border workers who have loans or investments in euros. On the other hand, if inflation stabilizes, the ECB could maintain rates unchanged, offering greater economic stability.

### Comparative table

| **Parameter** | Before the decision | After the decision |
|-----------------------------|---------------------------|----------------------------|
| Deposit rate | 1.75% | 2.00% |
| Refinancing rate | 2.00% | 2.15% |
| Marginal rate | 2.25% | 2.40% |
| Inflation | 2.0% | 2.0% (target) |
| Energy prices | Stable | Increased |

This table shows how interest rates have been kept unchanged despite the rise in energy prices. The ECB is closely monitoring the situation to avoid a negative impact on the economy.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### Concrete actions for cross-border workers

For cross-border workers who commute to Switzerland and live in Italy, it's crucial to closely monitor the evolution of interest rates and inflation. Here are some concrete steps that can be taken:

1. **Monitor energy costs**: With rising energy prices, it's essential to keep an eye on heating and transportation expenses. Consider using more affordable means of transport or adopting energy-efficient measures.
2. Review loans: If you have loans in euros, it's important to assess the impact of interest rates on credit costs. Consider renegotiating loan terms or seeking more advantageous financing options.
3. Invest cautiously: With inflation on the rise, it's important to diversify investments and consider options that offer protection against inflation. Consult a financial advisor to evaluate the best options.
4. Plan expenses: With purchasing power declining, it's important to carefully plan expenses and avoid unnecessary purchases. Creating and sticking to a detailed budget can help manage finances more effectively.

### Useful tools

For cross-border workers who want to monitor the evolution of interest rates and inflation, you can use the [interest calculator](nav:calculator) available on the site. This tool allows you to calculate the impact of interest rates on loans and investments, providing a clear view of your financial situation.

Additionally, you can consult the guides and resources available on the site to obtain further information on how to manage your finances in a context of high inflation and variable interest rates. Don't forget to use the [health insurance comparator](nav:health) to find the coverage best suited to your needs.

### Conclusion

The ECB's decision to keep interest rates unchanged has significant implications for cross-border workers who commute to Switzerland and live in Italy. It's important to closely monitor the situation's evolution and adopt concrete measures to manage finances effectively. Use the tools available on the site to get a clear view of your financial situation and make informed decisions.

*Source: [varesenoi.it](https://www.varesenoi.it/2026/04/30/leggi-notizia/articolo/la-bce-mantiene-invariati-i-tassi-dinteresse-1.html)*

item.title: Railway: progress in infrastructure expansion
item.description: 300 projects underway to expand the Swiss railway network by 2035, with work ongoing in Lausanne and Fribourg.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- 300 projects underway for railway expansion
- Work in progress in Lausanne and Fribourg
- Priorities redefined for financial reasons

## Key facts
- **What**: Expansion of the Swiss railway network
- When: Projects underway for the 2025 and 2035 phases
- Where: Switzerland, with a focus on Lausanne, Fribourg, Basel, and Eastern Switzerland
- Who: Federal Office of Transport (FOT) and SBB
- Amount: Not yet specified

Last year, Swiss companies achieved important milestones in the expansion of the railway network. This is what emerges from the latest report on the state of the works published today by the Federal Office of Transport (FOT). Currently, there are around 300 projects in the design or implementation phase as part of the 2025 and 2035 expansion phases and the future infrastructure development program.

In a statement, the FOT lists a series of construction sites. The largest project in the expansion program concerns the Lausanne station, where SBB started work in 2025. At Fribourg station, the platforms have been completed. SBB has put into service on schedule the first major infrastructure measure of the 2035 expansion phase, which allows for an intensified offer in the Laufental (BL).

### Impact on cross-border commuters

The expansion of the Swiss railway network will have a significant impact on cross-border commuters who cross the Italian-Swiss border daily to go to work. With the intensification of railway services, travel times between major Swiss cities and Italian border areas could be reduced, improving the quality of life of cross-border workers.

In particular, projects in the Basel area and Eastern Switzerland, which allow for a continuous half-hourly frequency for the IC Zurich-Chur, represent an important step forward for cross-border workers in these regions. These infrastructure improvements could encourage greater use of public transport, reducing vehicular traffic and pollution along major roads.

### Comparison with the previous situation

Before the start of these projects, cross-border commuters often had to rely on private transport or less frequent railway services, with longer and less predictable travel times. The expansion of the railway network and the intensification of services represent a tangible improvement over the previous situation, offering cross-border workers more efficient and sustainable travel options.

### What to do to take advantage of the new services

To take advantage of the new railway services, cross-border commuters can start planning their trips taking into account the new frequencies and updated schedules. It is advisable to regularly consult the websites of SBB and local operators to stay updated on any changes or improvements in services.

Furthermore, cross-border commuters can consider purchasing railway subscriptions that offer discounted rates for frequent travel. These subscriptions can represent a convenient investment for those who regularly use the train to commute to work, reducing transport costs in the long term.

### Next steps

The Federal Council intends to gradually develop the railway network and, for financial reasons, the priorities of the projects must be redefined. Based on an expert opinion from the Federal Institute of Technology in Zurich and the analyses of the competent Federal Offices, on January 28 last year the Government set the key points in this regard and the project to be put out for consultation is being elaborated, the adoption of which is planned by the beginning of the summer holidays.

For cross-border commuters, these developments represent an opportunity to improve their quality of life and reduce travel times. It is important to stay updated on the progress of the projects and the new opportunities offered by the expansion of the Swiss railway network. ## Practical analysis: implications for cross-border workers

The expansion of the Swiss railway network will have a significant impact on cross-border workers who daily cross the Italian-Swiss border to go to work. With the intensification of rail services, travel times between major Swiss cities and Italian border areas could be reduced, improving the quality of life for cross-border workers.

### Comparison with the previous situation

Before the start of these projects, cross-border workers often had to rely on private transport or less frequent rail services, with longer and less predictable travel times. The expansion of the railway network and the intensification of services represent a tangible improvement over the previous situation, offering cross-border workers more efficient and sustainable travel options.

### What to do to take advantage of the new services

To take advantage of the new rail services, cross-border workers can start planning their trips taking into account the new frequencies and updated schedules. It is advisable to regularly consult the websites of the FFS and local operators to stay updated on any changes or improvements in services.

In addition, cross-border workers can consider purchasing rail passes that offer discounted rates for frequent trips. These passes can represent a convenient investment for those who regularly use the train to commute to work, reducing transportation costs in the long run.

### Next steps

The Federal Council intends to gradually develop the railway network and, for financial reasons, the priorities of the projects must be redefined. Based on an expert opinion from the Swiss Federal Institute of Technology in Zurich and analyses by the competent federal offices, on January 28 last year the Government set the key points in this regard and the project to be put out for consultation is being elaborated, the adoption of which is planned by the beginning of the summer holidays.

For cross-border workers, these developments represent an opportunity to improve their quality of life and reduce travel times. It is important to stay updated on the progress of the projects and the new opportunities offered by the expansion of the Swiss railway network.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## Action: what to do concretely

To take advantage of the new rail services, cross-border workers can follow these steps:

1. **Plan your trips**: Consult the updated schedules of the FFS and local operators to plan your trips efficiently.
2. Buy subscriptions: Consider purchasing rail subscriptions that offer discounted rates for frequent travel.
3. Stay updated: Monitor the websites of the FFS and local operators to stay informed about any changes or improvements in the services.
4. Use public transport: Take advantage of the new rail services to reduce the use of private transport and contribute to environmental sustainability.

### Useful tools

For more information and to plan your trips, cross-border workers can use the following tools:

- [Salary calculator](nav:calculator)
- [Permit comparator](nav:permit-compare)
- [Tax calendar](nav:calendar)

These tools can help cross-border workers manage their trips better and stay updated on the latest news regarding the Swiss rail network.

*Source: [tio.ch](https://www.tio.ch/svizzera/attualita/1922126/progetti-corso-ampliamento-svizzera-progettazione)*

item.title: Record passenger numbers on Swiss trains in 2026
item.description: Major Swiss rail companies report a 5% increase in the first quarter of 2026, while freight transport drops by 4%.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Record number of passengers on Swiss trains in 2026
- 5% increase in the first quarter
- 4% drop in freight transport

## Key facts
- **What**: Record number of passengers on Swiss trains
- When: First quarter 2026
- Where: Switzerland
- Who: Swiss railway companies
- Amount: 5.79 billion passenger-kilometers

Major Swiss railway companies recorded a record number of passengers in the first quarter of 2026. According to the Public Transport Information Service (LITRA), passenger transport reached 5.79 billion passenger-kilometers, a 5.0% increase compared to the same period the previous year. However, compared to the previous quarter (October-December 2025), the figure is down 5.2%, reflecting a seasonal trend.

### Data analysis
The 5.2% drop compared to the previous quarter is due to lower demand in the early months of the year, when leisure time, shopping, and commuting are less frequent than at the end of the year. This phenomenon is normal and is confirmed by LITRA.

### Impact on freight transport
For its part, rail freight transport remains in difficulty even at the start of 2026. With 2.74 billion tonne-kilometers, the volume is down 4.0% year-on-year and 0.4% compared to the last quarter of 2025. This is the worst result for a first quarter since records began in 2014, a sign that the recovery is slow to come. In addition to the European economic slowdown, structural problems, capacity limits, and the complex management of traffic along the main logistics corridors are weighing heavily.

### Companies involved
For passenger transport, LITRA relied on data provided by the railway companies FFS, BLS, MGB (Matterhorn Gotthard Bahn), MOB (Montreux–Oberland bernese), RegionAlps (regional transport in Valais), Rhaetian Railway, SBB GmbH (cross-border regional traffic in Basel and Schaffhausen), SOB (Südostbahn), Thurbo (regional transport in eastern Switzerland), TILO, TPF (regional transport in Fribourg), TransN (regional transport in Neuchâtel), and Zentralbahn (Lucerne – Interlaken/Engelberg). For freight traffic, the following companies were considered: BLS Cargo, DB Cargo, RailCare, FFS Cargo, FFS Cargo International, Sersa, TR Trans Rail, TX Logistik, WRS.

### Implications for cross-border workers
This record number of passengers could have a significant impact on cross-border workers who use trains to travel between Italy and Switzerland. With the increase in the number of passengers, there could be greater congestion at border stations such as Chiasso and Gaggiolo, making it necessary to improve services and infrastructure to ensure efficient and comfortable mobility.

### Comparison with freight transport
While passenger transport records a record, rail freight transport continues to decline. This could indicate an increasing preference for road transport, which could have an impact on the environment and air quality, especially in urban areas and along the main logistics corridors.

### Conclusions
The record number of passengers on Swiss trains in the first quarter of 2026 is a positive sign for the public transport sector, but the decline in rail freight transport represents a challenge that requires attention and investment to improve the efficiency and capacity of infrastructure. ## Analysis of the implications
The record number of passengers on Swiss trains in the first quarter of 2026 has several implications for cross-border workers and the economy of the Canton of Ticino. The 5% increase compared to the previous year indicates a growing preference for rail transport, which could be due to various factors, including rising fuel prices, greater environmental awareness, and the expansion of railway infrastructure.

### Impact on cross-border workers
For cross-border workers who commute to Switzerland and live in Italy, the increase in train passengers could mean greater crowds at border stations such as Chiasso and Gaggiolo. This could lead to longer waiting times and a greater need to improve services and infrastructure to ensure efficient and comfortable mobility.

### Comparison with freight transport
While passenger transport is setting records, rail freight transport continues to decline. This could indicate an increasing preference for road transport, which could impact the environment and air quality, especially in urban areas and along major logistics corridors. Furthermore, the decline in freight transport could negatively affect the economy of the Canton of Ticino, which partly depends on the transport of goods through its border crossings.

### Necessary investments
To address these challenges, it is necessary to invest in railway infrastructure to improve the efficiency and capacity of freight transport. This could include the expansion of marshalling yards, the introduction of advanced technologies for traffic management, and an increase in the frequency of freight trains. Additionally, it may be necessary to promote incentives for companies that choose rail transport over road transport.

### Opportunities for the Canton of Ticino
The increase in the number of train passengers could represent an opportunity for the Canton of Ticino to further develop its railway infrastructure and improve the quality of the services offered. This could include the expansion of stations, the introduction of new services, and an increase in the frequency of trains. Furthermore, it may be necessary to promote awareness campaigns to encourage cross-border workers to use rail transport for their daily commutes.

### Comparison with other means of transport
While rail transport is setting passenger records, it is important to compare its advantages with those of other means of transport, such as cars and buses. Rail transport offers numerous advantages, including reduced CO2 emissions, greater transport capacity, and greater energy efficiency. However, it may be necessary to address some challenges, such as longer waiting times and the need to improve infrastructure to ensure efficient and comfortable mobility.

### Conclusions
The increase in the number of passengers on Swiss trains in the first quarter of 2026 represents an opportunity for the Canton of Ticino to further develop its railway infrastructure and improve the quality of the services offered. However, it is necessary to address the challenges related to freight transport and promote incentives to encourage companies to choose rail transport over road transport. Furthermore, it may be necessary to promote awareness campaigns to encourage cross-border workers to use rail transport for their daily commutes.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## Concrete actions for cross-border workers
For cross-border workers who use trains to travel between Italy and Switzerland, it's important to be aware of the implications of the passenger record registered in the first quarter of 2026. Here are some concrete actions that can be taken to address the challenges related to rail transport.

### Trip planning
With the increase in the number of passengers, it may be necessary to plan trips further in advance to avoid prolonged waiting times. Using apps and real-time information services can help monitor attendance and train delays, allowing for better organization of your trips.

### Use of alternative means of transport
In some cases, it may be necessary to consider the use of alternative means of transport, such as buses or cars, especially in case of particularly high attendance. However, it is important to evaluate the costs and travel times to choose the most convenient option.

### Promotion of rail transport
To encourage greater use of rail transport, it is important to promote its advantages over other means of transport. This could include awareness campaigns, incentives for commuters and improvements to railway infrastructure.

### Participation in public consultations
Cross-border workers can participate in public consultations and local initiatives to express their opinions and suggestions on how to improve rail transport. This can help influence the decisions of the authorities and promote solutions that meet the needs of commuters.

### Use of calculation tools
To plan your trips as best as possible and compare travel costs and times, you can use calculation tools such as the [travel calculator](nav:calculator). This tool can help compare the different transport options and choose the most convenient one based on your needs.

### Conclusions
The increase in the number of passengers on Swiss trains in the first quarter of 2026 represents a challenge for cross-border workers who use rail transport for their daily commutes. However, with careful planning and the use of calculation tools, it is possible to address these challenges and choose the most convenient transport option. Moreover, participation in public consultations and local initiatives can contribute to improving rail transport and promoting solutions that meet the needs of commuters.

*Source: [tvsvizzera.it](https://www.tvsvizzera.it/tvs/qui-svizzera/litra-ferrovie-record-viaggiatori-in-primo-trimestre/91343084)*

item.title: Swiss Competition Commission probes search engine ads: travel and online casinos
item.description: The Swiss Competition Commission opens two investigations into illegal agreements between travel sector companies and online casinos
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- COMCO investigates illicit agreements between travel sector companies and online casinos
- Companies refrain from competing on keywords in search engines
- Possible negative effects for consumers

## Key facts
- **What**: Investigations into search engine advertising
- When: April 30, 2026
- Where: Switzerland
- Who: Competition Commission (COMCO)
- Amount: Not specified

The Competition Commission (COMCO) has opened two investigations into search engine advertising. The first concerns three companies offering tourist packages in Switzerland, while the second involves almost all online casinos. Companies can bid on search engines for certain keywords, thus influencing the visibility of their offers. However, COMCO has received information that several companies in the travel sector and numerous online casinos have agreed not to compete on major search engines, refraining from bidding on certain keywords related to competing brands.

This approach could constitute an illicit agreement and have harmful effects on competition and consumers. According to COMCO, comparing offers from competitors could become more difficult for consumers. COMCO received this information as part of self-disclosures, suggesting that some companies themselves have recognized the possible illegality of their agreements.

### Implications for consumers

COMCO's investigations could have important implications for Swiss consumers, particularly those looking for tourist packages or online casino services. If companies agree not to compete, consumers may not see all available options, which could limit their ability to find the best offers. This could also lead to higher prices and lower quality of services offered.

### Investigation procedure

COMCO's investigations follow a rigorous procedure. The commission collects evidence and interviews the parties involved to determine if any wrongdoing has occurred. If evidence of illicit agreements is found, COMCO can impose sanctions on the companies involved. Sanctions may include fines and the obligation to immediately cease illegal practices.

### Next steps

The investigations are still ongoing, and COMCO has not yet made the results public. However, it is clear that the commission is taking these accusations very seriously and is working to protect the interests of Swiss consumers. Consumers can expect further updates as the investigations progress.

For more information on COMCO's investigations and how they might affect consumers, visit our [savings calculator](nav:calculator). ## Analysis of the implications for cross-border workers

The COMCO investigations could have a significant impact on cross-border workers who work in Switzerland and use travel services or online casino services. Cross-border workers often look for advantageous offers for travel and leisure, and the lack of competition could limit their options. This could influence their ability to find the best deals and save money.

### Comparison with the previous situation

Before the investigations, consumers, including cross-border workers, could count on a wide range of travel options and online casino services. The competition among companies ensured competitive prices and a higher quality of services. With the ongoing investigations, it is possible that this competition has been limited, which could lead to higher prices and lower quality of services.

### Possible scenarios

If the investigations confirm that the companies have agreed not to compete, the COMCO could impose sanctions and force the companies to resume competition. This could lead to a return to the previous situation, with greater competition and better offers for consumers. However, if the companies continue to avoid competing, consumers may have to face higher prices and lower quality of services for a longer period.

### Implications for cross-border workers

For cross-border workers, it is important to be aware of these investigations and their potential implications. Cross-border workers should monitor the developments of the investigations and consider their travel options and online casino services carefully. They may want to look for offers from other sources or consider alternatives to save money.

For more information on the implications for cross-border workers, visit our [municipality map](nav:border-map).

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## Concrete actions for consumers

Consumers, including cross-border workers, can take several concrete actions to protect themselves from the potential consequences of the COMCO investigations. Here are some steps they can follow:

1. Monitor the developments of the investigations: Stay up-to-date with the latest news and updates from the COMCO investigations. This can help better understand the potential implications and make informed decisions.
2. Seek offers from different sources: Do not limit yourself to a single source for finding travel offers or online casino services. Search on different websites and compare offers to find the best options.
3. Consider alternatives: If the available offers are not satisfactory, consider alternatives such as traveling to other regions or using online casino services from other countries.
4. Use comparison tools: Use online comparison tools to find the best offers and save money. These tools can help compare prices and services quickly and easily.

### Useful tools

To help consumers find the best offers and save money, Frontaliere Ticino offers a series of useful tools. These tools can help compare prices and services, find advantageous offers, and make informed decisions.

For more information on how to find the best offers and save money, visit our [price comparator](nav:cost-of-living).

*Source: [rsi.ch](https://www.rsi.ch/s/3705801)*

item.title: Passport service at 7,500 Italian post offices
item.description: From March 2024, passport issuance and renewal services are available at 7,500 Italian post offices, including those in the Polis project.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Passport available at 7,500 Italian post offices
- 206,000 passports issued, 156,000 in Polis municipalities
- Service launched in March 2024, completed on schedule

## Key facts
- **What**: Passport issuance and renewal
- When: From March 2024
- Where: 7,500 post offices in Italy
- Who: Poste Italiane, Ministry of the Interior, Ministry of Enterprises and Made in Italy
- Amount: 206,000 passports issued, 156,000 in Polis municipalities

The passport can be requested at approximately 7,500 post offices throughout Italy. This service, launched in March 2024 with a trial in the province of Bologna, has been completed on schedule and now covers the entire national territory. Post offices have issued around 206,000 passports, of which 156,000 in Polis municipalities. Thanks to the Polis project, citizens can obtain passports without having to go to the police station and with the possibility of receiving the document at home, a choice made by 8 out of 10 people living in small towns.

### Implications for cross-border workers
For cross-border workers living in Italy and working in Switzerland, this service represents a significant advantage. The possibility of requesting the passport at a nearby post office significantly reduces travel time and costs. Moreover, the option to receive the document at home is an additional benefit, especially for those with demanding work schedules or difficulties in traveling.

### Comparison with the previous situation
Before this service was introduced, cross-border workers had to go to the police stations to request or renew their passports. This often involved long waiting times and not always easy travel, especially for those living in peripheral areas or with poor connectivity. With the new service, the procedure has become much simpler and more accessible.

### Economic and social impact
The Polis project has a significant economic impact, with over 1 billion euros contributed to the GDP, 18,500 jobs created, and 484 million euros generated from labor income. This demonstrates how the initiative not only simplifies citizens' lives but also contributes to the country's economic growth and social cohesion.

### Procedure to request a passport
To request a passport at a post office, you need to book an appointment online or go directly to one of the authorized offices. It is important to bring the necessary documents with you, such as your ID card, tax code, and a recent photograph. Once the application is submitted, the passport will be delivered to your home or to the post office, depending on your preference.

### Conclusion
The passport issuance and renewal service at post offices represents a significant step forward for Italian citizens, and in particular for cross-border workers. The simplification of procedures and the greater accessibility of services contribute to improving the quality of life and supporting social and territorial cohesion. ## Practical Analysis
The new passport issuance and renewal service at post offices offers numerous practical advantages for cross-border workers. Firstly, the possibility of avoiding long queues at police headquarters represents a precious time saving. Moreover, the possibility of receiving the document at home further reduces travel and associated costs.

### Comparison with other services
Compared to other document issuance services, such as the procedures for residence permits, the service offered by Poste Italiane stands out for its simplicity and speed. The agreement stipulated between Poste Italiane, the Ministry of the Interior, and the Ministry of Enterprises and Made in Italy guarantees an efficient and reliable service.

### Concrete Scenarios
Imagine a cross-border worker residing in a small town in Northern Italy and working in Canton Ticino. Before the service was launched, to renew the passport, they had to go to the nearest police headquarters, often located several kilometers away. Now, they can simply go to the post office in their town, saving time and money.

### Impact on Daily Life
The simplification of bureaucratic procedures has a direct impact on the quality of life of cross-border workers. The possibility of managing administrative procedures in a simpler and faster way allows more time to be devoted to family and personal activities. Furthermore, the reduction in travel costs represents an additional advantage, especially in a complex economic context.

### Comparison with the Situation in Switzerland
In Switzerland, the issuance and renewal of passports are managed directly by the cantonal authorities. However, the service offered by Poste Italiane represents a model of efficiency that could be considered in Switzerland, especially for cross-border workers residing in Italy and working across the border.

### Conclusions
The passport issuance and renewal service at Italian post offices represents a virtuous example of bureaucratic simplification and efficiency of public services. For cross-border workers, this initiative represents a further step towards greater ease in managing administrative procedures, contributing to improving the quality of life and supporting social cohesion.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## Concrete action
For cross-border workers who wish to apply for or renew their passport at post offices, it is necessary to follow a few simple but fundamental steps. Here is a practical guide for those who want to request or renew their passport.

### Step 1: Verify the requirements
Before going to a post office, it is important to check that you have all the necessary documents. These include an identity card, tax code, and a recent photograph. It is also advisable to book an appointment online to avoid unnecessary waiting times.

### Step 2: Booking the appointment
The appointment can be booked online through the Poste Italiane website. You can choose the most convenient date and time, based on the availability of the chosen post office. The booking is free and can be done in a few minutes.

### Step 3: Going to the post office
On the day of the appointment, you need to go to the post office with all the required documents. It is important to arrive a few minutes early to avoid delays. During the appointment, a postal operator will register the application and provide all the necessary information.

### Step 4: Receiving the passport
Once the application has been submitted, the passport will be delivered to your home or to the post office, depending on the applicant's preference. The waiting time for delivery varies depending on the type of passport requested and the availability of the competent authorities.

### Using the salary calculator
For cross-border workers who want to estimate their net salary in Switzerland, you can use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator). This tool allows you to easily calculate the net salary in the payslip, taking into account taxes and social security contributions.

### Conclusion
The passport issuance and renewal service at post offices represents a concrete opportunity to simplify the management of administrative procedures. By following the steps indicated, cross-border workers can benefit from an efficient and reliable service, saving time and money.

*Source: [varesenoi.it](https://www.varesenoi.it/2026/04/30/leggi-notizia/articolo/il-passaporto-si-puo-richiedere-in-7500-uffici-postali-in-tutta-italia.html)*

item.title: SBB and Siemens sign contract for 116 new trains
item.description: SBB and Siemens Mobility Switzerland have signed the contract for 116 new double-decker trains for the S-Bahn, with an investment of up to 2 billion francs.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- SBB and Siemens sign for 116 new double-decker trains
- Investment up to 2 billion francs
- Service entry scheduled from 2031

## Key facts
- **What**: Contract for 116 new double-decker trains
- When: Recently signed, service entry from 2031
- Where: Switzerland, mainly Zurich and Western Switzerland
- Who: SBB and Siemens Mobility Switzerland
- Amount: Up to 2 billion francs

Swiss Federal Railways (SBB) and Siemens Mobility Switzerland have signed the contract for the supply of 116 new double-decker trains destined for the S-Bahn. This significant agreement, valued at up to 2 billion francs, marks a major milestone for Swiss rail transport. The new trains, which will enter service from 2031, will replace the first-generation models currently in use.

The new trains will offer a significantly higher capacity compared to current models, with around 540 seats. According to the railway companies, travelers will have 10% more seats and 30% more standing room. This improvement is particularly relevant for urban areas like Zurich, where demand for public transport is constantly increasing.

### Context and Challenges

The awarding of the contract was not without controversy. Initially, competitor Stadler Rail had contested the awarding of the contract to Siemens, filing an appeal. However, Stadler later withdrew the appeal, making the award to Siemens final. This withdrawal was communicated by SBB already at the beginning of April, thus allowing the signing of the contract.

The new trains will be distributed between the Zurich S-Bahn, which will receive 95 vehicles, and Western Switzerland, which will receive 21. This distribution aims to improve the quality of railway service in both regions, offering passengers a more comfortable and efficient travel experience.

### Impact on Cross-Border Workers

For cross-border workers who regularly use railway services for travel between the Canton of Ticino and other Swiss regions, the introduction of these new trains represents a significant improvement. The increased capacity and enhanced comfort will make travel more pleasant and practical, reducing waiting times and improving service quality.

Furthermore, the increase in seats and standing room will be particularly useful during peak hours when transport demand is highest. This improvement will contribute to making public transport an even more attractive choice for commuters, reducing the use of private vehicles and contributing to environmental sustainability.

### Future Prospects

The entry into service of the new trains is scheduled from 2031, but planning and preparation work is already underway. SBB and Siemens will work closely together to ensure that the new trains are delivered on schedule and fully operational when they enter service.

This project represents a significant investment in the future of Swiss rail transport and an important step towards the modernization of infrastructure. With the increasing demand for public transport, the introduction of these new trains will be fundamental to meeting passenger needs and improving the quality of service offered. ## Analysis of Implications

The introduction of new double-decker trains by FFS and Siemens Mobility Switzerland will have a significant impact on Swiss public transport, particularly for cross-border workers who regularly use rail services. This project will not only improve train capacity and comfort but also contribute to environmental sustainability and reduced road traffic.

### Greater Capacity and Comfort

One of the main advantages of the new trains is their increased capacity. With approximately 540 seats, these trains will offer 10% more seating and 30% more standing room compared to first-generation models. This increased capacity will be particularly useful during peak hours when demand for public transport is highest.

For cross-border workers who travel daily between the Canton of Ticino and other Swiss regions, this greater capacity means less waiting time and more comfort during the journey. The new trains will be equipped with advanced technologies and improved comfort, making the trip more pleasant and practical.

### Environmental Impact

The increased capacity of the trains will also contribute to environmental sustainability. With more passengers choosing public transport over private vehicles, road traffic and CO2 emissions will be reduced. This is particularly important in a context where Switzerland is making significant efforts to reduce greenhouse gas emissions and promote sustainable mobility.

### Comparison with the Previous Situation

Before the introduction of the new trains, first-generation models were often overcrowded, especially during peak hours. This led to discomfort for passengers and a reduction in service quality. With the new trains, a significant improvement in the situation is expected, with less crowding and greater service efficiency.

### Future Scenarios

With the new trains scheduled to enter service in 2031, FFS and Siemens are already working to ensure everything is ready by that date. This includes route planning, staff training, and the maintenance of necessary infrastructure. The new trains are expected to be a success, improving service quality and meeting passenger needs.

### Conclusion

The introduction of new double-decker trains by FFS and Siemens Mobility Switzerland represents an important step towards the modernization of Swiss rail transport. With increased capacity, improved comfort, and a positive impact on the environment, these trains will offer numerous benefits for passengers, particularly for cross-border workers who regularly use rail services. This project is a significant investment in the future of Swiss public transport and an important step towards sustainability.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## Actions and Procedure

With the introduction of new double-decker trains by FFS and Siemens Mobility Switzerland, it is important that passengers, especially cross-border workers, are informed about the procedures and necessary actions to make the most of these improvements. Here is a step-by-step guide on what to do and what to expect.

### Step 1: Get Informed About New Routes

FFS will soon publish the new routes and train schedules. It is important for cross-border workers to stay informed about these changes to plan their trips efficiently. Information will be available on the FFS website and through their official apps.

### Step 2: Use Official Apps

The official FFS apps provide real-time information on train schedules, any delays, and other useful news. It is advisable to download and use these apps to stay updated on the latest news and to plan trips optimally.

### Step 3: Plan Trips in Advance

With the increased capacity of the trains, there may be changes in schedules and routes. It is advisable to plan trips in advance to avoid surprises and to ensure timely arrival at the destination. FFS will provide online planning tools to help passengers organize their trips.

### Step 4: Use Subscription Services

FFS offers various types of subscriptions that can be advantageous for cross-border workers who regularly use railway services. It is advisable to get informed about these subscriptions and evaluate if they can be useful for your travel needs.

### Step 5: Attend Informative Events

FFS will organize informative events to present the new trains and answer passengers' questions. It is advisable to attend these events to get direct information and clarify any doubts.

### Step 6: Use the Salary Calculator

For cross-border workers who want to calculate their net salary after tax deductions, you can use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) available on the Frontaliere Ticino website. This tool helps to better understand withholding taxes and other tax aspects.

### Step 7: Contact Customer Support

In case of questions or problems, you can contact FFS customer support to get assistance. FFS offers various support channels, including phone, email, and online chat.

### Conclusion

The introduction of new double-decker trains by FFS and Siemens Mobility Switzerland represents an opportunity to improve the quality of Swiss public transport. By following these steps and using the available tools, cross-border workers can plan their trips efficiently and make the most of the new services offered. For more information and to use the salary calculator, visit the [Frontaliere Ticino](nav:calculator) website.
