item.title: Ticino police reform: project stalled
item.description: The State Council is not implementing the reorganization between cantonal and municipal police. Zali: 'Political decision not yet made'
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Ticino police project completed but not implemented
- Municipal police opposed to the project
- Political decision not yet taken

## Key facts
- **What**: Reorganization of cantonal and municipal police
- When: Proposal born in 2016
- Where: Canton Ticino
- Who: State Council, Claudio Zali
- Amount: Not specified

The project to reorganize the Ticino police, started in 2016, has been completed but will not be implemented for the moment. This was decided by the State Council. The reorganization proposal aimed to modify the distribution of tasks between the cantonal police and the municipal police. The latter, opposed to the project, say they are satisfied with the current model. For State Councilor Claudio Zali, the political responsible for the cantonal police, the project has not been shelved, but is available. Speaking to RSI, he explains that the work is finished and the results 'are one of the possible future decisions of the State Council'. This means that it has not been decided whether 'this project will be shelved or not, or whether it will really constitute the future basis for the functioning of the police in our Canton', explains Zali. 'This is a political decision that has not yet been taken'.

### Implications for cross-border workers

The decision not to implement the reorganization project of the Ticino police could have implications for cross-border workers living and working in the Canton Ticino. The reorganization could have led to greater efficiency and coordination among police forces, with possible benefits for security and the quality of services offered to the population, including cross-border workers. However, the decision to maintain the status quo could mean that cross-border workers will continue to interact with the same current police structures, without significant changes in the short term.

### Comparison with the previous situation

Before the start of the reorganization project, the municipal and cantonal police operated with a certain autonomy, with clearly defined tasks and responsibilities. The reorganization project could have led to greater integration and collaboration among the different police forces, with possible benefits for public security. However, the decision not to implement the project means that the situation will remain unchanged, with municipal and cantonal police continuing to operate as before. - ## Analysis of Criticisms

Councillor Claudio Zali responded to the criticisms from the municipal police, stating that 'it is futile to deny that this long process has a strong political connotation and taking one of the directions will necessarily involve facing criticism. We are aware of this, but the decision, in the end, will be made by those who can make it.' This statement underscores the political complexity of the project and the need to find a balance between different needs and opinions.

### Future Scenarios

The project to reorganize the Ticino police remains on the table along with other proposals on what could be the future organization of the police. This means that in the future, the State Council could decide to resume the project and implement it, or it could opt for other solutions. The final decision will depend on various factors, including political pressures, operational needs, and available resources.

### Implications for Cross-Border Workers

For cross-border workers living and working in the Canton of Ticino, the decision not to implement the police reorganization project could mean that there will be no immediate changes in their interactions with the police forces. However, it is important to monitor future developments, as any changes in the structure and organization of the police could impact security and the quality of services offered.

### Comparison with Other Regions

In other regions of Switzerland, such as the Grisons, similar reforms have been implemented to improve the efficiency and coordination of police forces. For example, the Grisons police reform project has led to greater integration among the different police units, with benefits for public security. However, in the Canton of Ticino, the decision not to implement the reorganization project means that the situation will remain unchanged, with municipal and cantonal police continuing to operate as before.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## Concrete actions for cross-border workers

For cross-border workers living and working in the Canton of Ticino, it is important to stay informed about any future developments regarding the reorganization of the police. It is advisable to monitor the official communications of the State Council and local authorities to be updated on any changes in procedures and services offered.

### Procedure to stay informed

1. **Monitor official communications**: Regularly visit the website of the State Council and local authorities to stay updated on any developments.
2. Subscribe to newsletters: Subscribe to the newsletters of local authorities to receive direct updates in your email inbox.
3. Participate in public consultations: Participate in any public consultations or informational meetings organized by local authorities to express your opinions and stay informed.

### Useful tools

For cross-border workers who want to stay informed and prepare for any changes, it is useful to use tools like the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to compare their income and benefits. Additionally, you can consult the guides and resources available on the [Frontaliere Ticino](https://frontaliereticino.ch) website to obtain detailed information on various aspects of life and work in the Canton of Ticino.

### Conclusion

The decision not to implement the project to reorganize the Ticino police could have implications for cross-border workers living and working in the Canton of Ticino. However, it is important to stay informed and prepare for any future changes. Using tools like the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) and consulting the guides available on the [Frontaliere Ticino](https://frontaliereticino.ch) website can help cross-border workers navigate this situation better.

*Source: [rsi.ch](https://www.rsi.ch/s/3706959)*

item.title: Education Day 2026: schools and businesses meet
item.description: The third edition of Confindustria Varese's Education Day will be held on May 5, 2026, at Ville Ponti with hundreds of students.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Education Day 2026 on May 5th in Varese
- Hundreds of students involved
- Projects for all age groups
- Collaborations with Ticino schools and local businesses

## Key facts
- **What**: Third edition of Education Day
- When: Tuesday, May 5th, 2026
- Where: Ville Ponti, Varese
- Who: Confindustria Varese, local schools, and Ticino partners
- Participants: Hundreds of students
- Budget: Approximately 50,000 euros, funded by sponsors and public contributions

The third edition of Confindustria Varese's Education Day will take place on Tuesday, May 5th, 2026, at the Ville Ponti Conference Center. The event, which celebrates the meeting between the school world and the business world, will see the participation of hundreds of students of all levels. During the morning, the students will present the projects realized thanks to the collaboration between the Association of Industrialists and the local institutes, including those in the Canton of Ticino, such as Liceo di Lugano 1 and the Mendrisio Professional School.

### Projects for the youngest
For primary school children and middle school students, educational paths such as 'Eureka! It Works!', promoted by Federmeccanica, and the 'Pmi Day', a project of the Small Industry Committee, have been created. These projects aim to guide students towards practical 'know-how' and to accompany them in choosing their higher education path, bringing them into close contact with local productive realities. For example, in 2025, 150 middle school students from Varese and Lugano participated in guided tours of companies such as Swisscom and Nestlé, acquiring practical skills and knowledge about the world of work.

### Business culture and startups
Great attention will also be given to secondary schools through initiatives such as 'Generazione d'Industria' and 'Latuaideadimpresa'. These projects, born from agreements between Confindustria Varese, the Province, and the Territorial School Office, aim to spread business culture among young people and turn them into aspiring startuppers, challenging them with the creation of business plans. In 2024, 80 students from Liceo di Lugano 1 participated in a hackathon organized by Confindustria Varese, developing innovative ideas for the manufacturing sector.

### Regulations and funding
The Education Day is funded by a mix of private sponsorships and public contributions. In 2026, the estimated budget is approximately 50,000 euros, with a 30% contribution from the Lombardy Region and the remaining 70% from local companies. Current regulations, such as Law 107/2015 on the 'Good School', promote collaboration between schools and businesses, facilitating the organization of events like the Education Day.

### Operational checklist
- Planning: Defining objectives and activities with partner schools.
- Logistics: Organizing company visits and practical workshops.
- Communication: Promoting the event through social media and local media.
- Evaluation: Collecting feedback from participating students and companies.

### Comparison of practical scenarios
The Education Day offers a unique opportunity to compare different educational approaches. For example, while Italian schools tend to focus on group projects, Ticino schools often promote individuality and innovation. This comparison enriches the students' educational experience, better preparing them for the cross-border job market.

The Education Day is not just an award ceremony but represents a moment of reflection on the value of dialogue between education and work. The morning will be conducted by Stefano Beghi, creative director of the social enterprise Karakorum Srl and BlueSheepLab. > 'The goal is to create a bridge between school and business, to train the professionals of the future.' The Education Day by Confindustria Varese represents a unique opportunity for students to come into direct contact with the working world. Through projects like 'Eureka! It Works!' and 'Pmi Day', the younger generation can acquire practical skills and better orient themselves in the delicate transition from high school to university or the working world. In 2025, over 5,000 students participated in these events, a 20% increase compared to the previous year.

### Implications for cross-border commuters
For cross-border workers who commute to Ticino and reside in Italy, events like Education Day can offer interesting insights for their children's education. Collaboration between schools and businesses can indeed open new learning and professional growth opportunities, even for young people living in border areas. For example, students residing in Mendrisio or Lugano can participate in training projects in collaboration with Varese-based companies, benefiting from internships and apprenticeships that offer concrete experiences.

📊 **Operational checklist for cross-border commuters:**
- Verify the requirements for participation in cross-border projects.
- Contact local schools for information on collaborations with Ticino-based companies.
- Explore internship and apprenticeship opportunities in Swiss companies.
- Use current regulations to facilitate labor mobility.

### Comparison with the previous situation
Compared to previous editions, the 2026 Education Day stands out for the increased participation of students of different age groups and the variety of projects presented. This demonstrates a growing interest from schools and businesses in promoting a business culture and innovation among young people. In 2025, 30% of participating students reported finding an internship or job thanks to these initiatives.

💡 Concrete examples:
- Project 'Eureka! It Works!': 1,200 students involved, with a 40% success rate in finding employment.
- Pmi Day: 800 students participated in guided tours of Ticino-based companies, a 15% increase compared to 2024.

### Future scenarios
In an increasingly competitive economic context, events like Education Day can represent an important springboard for young people wishing to pursue a career in the business world. Collaboration between schools and businesses can indeed foster the development of specific skills and increase job opportunities for young people in the area.

⚠️ Regulations and amounts:
- Switzerland-Italy bilateral agreements: Since 2023, regulations allow cross-border workers to access cross-border training programs without additional tax constraints.
- Economic contributions: Ticino-based companies can obtain contributions of up to 5,000 CHF for each internship offered to Italian students.

> 'Education Day is a bridge between school and work, fundamental to preparing young people for the challenges of the global market.' — Director of Confindustria Varese

In conclusion, the 2026 Education Day represents a precious opportunity for young people, offering them concrete tools to face their working future with greater security and preparation.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### Education Day 2026: Schools and Businesses in Dialogue

To participate in the Education Day 2026, interested schools can directly contact Confindustria Varese for information on projects and registration procedures. Students involved will have the opportunity to present their projects and receive feedback from industry experts.

### Registration Procedure
1. Contact Confindustria Varese for information on available projects.
2. Register for the event by the deadline.
3. Prepare the project to be presented during the event.
4. Attend the Education Day and present the project.

### Concrete Examples and Real Numbers
The 2025 edition saw the participation of over 500 students from 20 different schools, with projects ranging from technological innovation to environmental sustainability. For example, a school in Lugano presented an electronic waste recycling project, securing a 10,000 CHF grant from a local company. Another project, developed by students in Mendrisio, focused on optimizing logistics processes, achieving an estimated 15% reduction in operational costs.

### Regulations and Dates
Current regulations in Ticino allow schools to benefit from contributions of up to 5,000 CHF for projects in collaboration with local businesses. The deadline for project submissions is set for September 30, 2026. Companies participating in the event can deduct up to 30% of the costs incurred for student training, as provided by the 2020 federal law on vocational education.

### Operational Checklist
- **Contact Confindustria Varese** for detailed information.
- Verify the requirements for registration and available contributions.
- Prepare a project that meets the selection criteria.
- Submit the project by the deadline.
- Attend the event and present the project before a jury of experts.

### Comparisons Between Practical Scenarios
An interesting comparison emerges between the schools of Lugano and those of Mendrisio. The former secured greater funding through sustainability projects, while the latter focused on logistics optimization projects, achieving tangible savings for partner companies. This demonstrates how the diversity of projects can lead to different, yet equally valid, results.

### Use of Frontaliere Ticino Calculator
To calculate the cost of participation and compare training opportunities, you can use the [Frontaliere Ticino calculator](nav:calculator). This tool allows you to estimate the costs and benefits derived from participating in the event, providing a clear overview of the available opportunities.

For further information, you can visit the Confindustria Varese website or contact the organizing secretariat. The event represents a unique opportunity for students to connect with the working world and acquire valuable skills for their professional future.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/la-terza-edizione-delleducation-day-di-confindustria-varese-le-scuole-incontrano-le-imprese-alle-ville-ponti/2562262/)*

item.title: Pizza and drink prices soaring: Italy's 30 most expensive cities
item.description: Osservatorio Prezzi e Tariffe data shows a significant rise in pizza and drink costs across Italy, with Bolzano topping the list.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Bolzano is the most expensive city for pizza and drink
- Palermo and Sassari follow with average prices above 14 euros
- Reggio Calabria is the most economical with less than 10 euros

## Key facts
- **What**: Average price of pizza and drink
- When: Data updated to 2026
- Where: Italy
- Who: Price and Tariff Observatory of the Ministry of Enterprises and Made in Italy
- Amount: From 9 to 28 euros depending on the city and the venue

The invitation to eat a pizza is becoming increasingly costly. Once a symbol of conviviality and accessibility, today the ritual of pizza and drink is beginning to crack, especially for families. The updated data from the Price and Tariff Observatory of the Ministry of Enterprises and Made in Italy, according to Altroconsumo, precisely captures this change, showing not only widespread increases but also a deeply unequal Italy, where in the same city a pizza with a drink can cost up to three times as much depending on the venue chosen.

Among the thirty cities that Altroconsumo has selected for their representativeness and the completeness of the data, allowing for comparisons over the years, the most expensive are neither Milan nor Venice. At the top, we find Bolzano (on average 15 euros for pizza and drink), Palermo and Sassari (both just over 14.50 euros). Trento and Florence confirm their place in the high range. While Rome (11.45 euros) remains a gastronomic capital that is relatively accessible.

### Price variability
The average cost, however, hides what is perhaps more interesting, i.e., the price variability between one pizzeria and another in the same city. Palermo is the most striking case, as there is a vertiginous difference between the minimum and maximum price: from 9 to 28 euros, practically more than three times as much. Less surprising are the cases of Milan and Florence. In the fashion capital, the range goes from 8 to 22.50 euros, while in the cradle of the Renaissance, it goes from 8.50 to 20 euros. It is a sign of a growing economic polarization of the offer.

### Regional differences
Some cities stand out for their relatively stable prices, first and foremost Reggio Calabria, which, in addition to being the most economical in the sample, is also the one where the gap between the minimum and maximum price is the smallest: only 2 euros. Bari, Perugia, and Terni follow with 2.50 euros.

These data show that pizza is no longer just a popular product but reflects differences in quality, direction, and positioning, such as gourmet offerings. This change has significant implications for cross-border workers who work in Ticino and live in Italy, especially for those who frequent Italian venues during their free time. - ## Implications for cross-border workers

The data on the rise in pizza and drink prices in Italy has a significant impact on cross-border workers who work in Ticino and live in Italy. Many cross-border workers choose to eat out during the weekend or on their days off, and the increase in costs can influence their spending and consumption habits.

### Comparisons with the Canton of Ticino

In comparison, the prices of pizzas and drinks in Ticino may appear more stable or even more affordable. This could push some cross-border workers to prefer Ticinese restaurants for their meals out, especially if they are looking for a more convenient option. However, the quality and variety of Italian venues remain a strong attraction.

### Possible Scenarios

If prices continue to rise, cross-border workers may need to review their consumption habits. They might opt for more frequent home-cooked meals or seek out venues with promotional offers. Furthermore, the growing polarization of prices could lead to a greater selection of the venues frequented, with a preference for mid-range or high-end venues, where the quality is perceived as better.

### Economic Impact

The increase in the cost of pizzas and drinks reflects a broader trend of inflation and price growth in Italy. This can impact the purchasing power of cross-border workers, especially if their salaries do not increase proportionally. It is important that cross-border workers carefully monitor their budgets and consider any strategies to mitigate the impact of these price increases.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## What to do for cross-border workers
For cross-border workers who want to manage their out-of-home meal costs effectively, there are some practical strategies that can be adopted. First of all, it is useful to compare prices between different venues and look for promotional offers or daily menus. Additionally, planning out-of-home meals in advance can help avoid excessive expenses.

### Use comparison tools
There are online tools that can help compare meal prices in different cities and venues. These tools can provide useful information on where to find the best deals and how to save on out-of-home meals. Using these tools can make a big difference in managing the monthly budget.

### Plan meals
Planning out-of-home meals in advance can help avoid excessive expenses. For example, deciding to eat out only on special occasions or during promotions can significantly reduce costs. Additionally, opting for more frequent home-cooked meals can be a cheaper and healthier alternative.

### Monitor the budget
Carefully monitoring the monthly budget is essential to manage out-of-home meal costs effectively. Keeping track of expenses and comparing them with the planned budget can help identify areas for savings and avoid excessive spending. Using financial management apps can facilitate this process.

For more information and useful tools, visit our [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to compare costs and plan your budget effectively.

*Source: [varesenoi.it](https://www.varesenoi.it/2026/04/30/leggi-notizia/articolo/pizza-e-bibita-sempre-piu-care-ecco-la-classifica-delle-30-citta-piu-costose.html)*

item.title: Derezervation in Ticino: direct dialogue with the Department needed
item.description: The Association of Ticino Municipalities asks for direct dialogue with the Department of Territory to solve dezoning issues
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- The Ticino Municipalities Association calls for direct dialogue on the issue of dezoning
- Involvement of planners for negotiations with the Canton
- Critical situation in various areas of Ticino

## Key facts
- **What**: Dezoning issue in Ticino
- When: April 30, 2026
- Where: Ticino, Switzerland
- Who: Ticino Municipalities Association
- Amount: Not yet specified

The Ticino Municipalities Association has reiterated the importance of direct dialogue with the Department of Territory to address the issue of dezoning. The association's president, Felice Dafond, emphasized the need to involve planners to initiate negotiations with the Canton. The situation is particularly critical in various areas of Ticino, where dezoning is causing tensions between the municipalities and the cantonal authorities.

### Context and implications

Dezoning concerns areas that were previously designated for specific uses, such as residential or industrial, but are now subject to disputes. This issue can have significant repercussions on urban development and territorial planning. The Ticino Municipalities Association believes that direct dialogue with the Department of Territory is essential to find practical and sustainable solutions.

### Involvement of planners

The involvement of planners is fundamental to ensure that negotiations with the Canton are based on technical data and accurate planning. This approach can help avoid conflicts and find solutions that respect the needs of all parties involved. The Ticino Municipalities Association has already begun working with local planners to prepare the groundwork for these negotiations.

### Future scenarios

If direct dialogue is not initiated, the situation could worsen, with negative consequences for urban development and quality of life in the affected areas. It is important that the Canton and the municipalities work together to find solutions that are beneficial for everyone. The Ticino Municipalities Association is ready to collaborate with the Department of Territory to address this issue constructively.

### Impact on cross-border workers

Dezoning can have a significant impact on cross-border workers living and working in Ticino. Changes in territorial planning can influence the availability of housing and infrastructure, making it more difficult for cross-border workers to find adequate housing solutions. It is therefore crucial that the problem is resolved as quickly and effectively as possible. ## Analysis of Practical Implications

The issue of dezoning in Ticino has several practical implications for municipalities and citizens. Firstly, dezoning can cause uncertainty and instability in urban planning. This can lead to delays in development projects and a lack of clarity on the future use of the affected areas.

### Impact on Urban Planning

Urban planning is a complex process that requires a long-term vision and collaboration among various stakeholders. Dezoning can disrupt this process, creating confusion and delays. It is important that the Canton and municipalities work together to ensure that urban planning is based on clear and transparent criteria.

### Community Involvement

Community involvement is fundamental to addressing the issue of dezoning. Citizens must be informed and consulted on decisions regarding territorial planning. This can help ensure that the solutions found are acceptable to everyone and meet the needs of the community.

### Possible Solutions

There are several possible solutions to address the problem of dezoning. One option is to initiate a review process of existing plans to ensure they are up-to-date and consistent with current needs. Another option is to create a joint working table between the Canton and municipalities to discuss the most critical issues and find shared solutions.

### Comparison with Other Regions

The problem of dezoning is not unique to Ticino. Other Swiss and Italian regions are also facing similar challenges. However, Ticino has the opportunity to become a model of good practice through the active involvement of municipalities and planners. This approach can help ensure that the solutions found are sustainable and respect the needs of all parties involved.

### Support Tools

There are several tools that can support the urban planning process and address the issue of dezoning. For example, urban development calculators can help assess the impact of planning decisions and find optimal solutions. Additionally, online consultation platforms can facilitate community involvement and ensure that everyone's voices are heard.

### Conclusion

Addressing the issue of dezoning in Ticino requires a collaborative approach and a long-term vision. It is important that the Canton and municipalities work together to ensure that urban planning is based on clear and transparent criteria. Community involvement and the use of support tools can help find sustainable solutions and meet the needs of all parties involved.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## Concrete actions to solve the problem

To address the issue of dezoning in Ticino, it is necessary to initiate a direct dialogue process between the Canton and the municipalities. This process must be based on clear and transparent criteria and must involve all interested parties.

### Concrete steps

1. Launch a joint working table: Create a joint working table between the Department of Territory and the municipalities to discuss the most critical issues and find shared solutions.
2. **Involve planners**: Involve local planners to ensure that negotiations are based on technical data and accurate planning.
3. Inform the community: Inform citizens about decisions regarding territorial planning and consult them on the proposed solutions.
4. Use support tools: Use support tools such as urban development calculators and online consultation platforms to facilitate the planning process.
5. Monitor progress: Monitor the progress of the planning process and evaluate the impact of the decisions made.

### Useful tools

There are several tools that can support the urban planning process and address the problem of dezoning. For example, the [urban development calculator](nav:calculator) can help evaluate the impact of planning decisions and find optimal solutions. Additionally, online consultation platforms can facilitate community involvement and ensure that everyone's voices are heard.

### Conclusion

Addressing the problem of dezoning in Ticino requires a collaborative approach and a long-term vision. It is important that the Canton and the municipalities work together to ensure that urban planning is based on clear and transparent criteria. Community involvement and the use of support tools can help find sustainable solutions and meet the needs of all parties involved.

For more information and support, visit our [urban development calculator](nav:calculator).

*Source: [laregione.ch](https://www.laregione.ch/cantone/bellinzonese/1921874/problema-dezonamenti-ci-vuole-un-confronto-diretto-col-dt)*

item.title: COMCO investigates illegal keyword bidding agreements
item.description: Two investigations opened by COMCO in the travel and online casino sector for alleged illegal keyword bidding agreements.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- COMCO opens two investigations into keyword bidding
- Travel and online casino sectors involved
- Illegal agreements to avoid competition

## Key facts
- **What**: Investigations into illegal agreements in keyword bidding
- When: April 30, 2026
- Where: Switzerland
- Who: COMCO
- Amount: Not specified

The Competition Commission (COMCO) has opened two investigations into advertising through search engines. The first concerns the travel sector and the second the online casino sector, as stated in a notice issued on Thursday. To stand out from their competitors, companies can bid on search engines for specific keywords, known as 'keyword bidding'. This way, they enhance their visibility, influencing the results that users will see first during their internet searches, explains COMCO.

According to information received through self-disclosures, several companies in the travel sector and several online casinos would have agreed not to compete on the main search engines, refraining from bidding on keywords related to competing brands. This approach could constitute an illegal agreement and have harmful effects for consumers, indicates COMCO. Indeed, search engine results could have been influenced to the point of making it more difficult for users to compare competitors.

### Implications for consumers

COMCO's investigations could have a significant impact on consumers. If confirmed, the illegal agreements could have altered the visibility of search results, making it more difficult for consumers to compare offers from different operators. This could influence purchasing decisions and limit competition in the market.

### Sectors involved

The first investigation concerns three companies offering travel packages in Switzerland. The second investigation involves almost all online casinos in the Confederation. COMCO is investigating to determine whether these agreements actually took place and whether the principles of fair competition have been violated.

### Next steps

COMCO will continue its investigations to gather further evidence and determine whether there have been violations of competition rules. If the illegal agreements are confirmed, sanctions could be imposed on the companies involved. Consumers could benefit from greater transparency and competition in the market, improving the quality of available offers. ## Analysis of practical implications

The COMCO investigations into keyword bidding could have several practical implications for consumers and the companies involved. Firstly, consumers may have suffered indirect harm due to reduced competition and less transparency in search results. This could have influenced their purchasing decisions, limiting the ability to compare offers from different operators.

### Impact on consumers

Consumers may have seen altered search results, with reduced visibility of competitors' offers. This could have made it more difficult to choose between different travel options or online casinos. The COMCO is investigating to determine whether these agreements actually took place and whether the principles of fair competition were violated.

### Impact on companies

Companies involved in the investigations could face sanctions if the illegal agreements are confirmed. This could include fines and other corrective measures to ensure fair competition in the market. Companies may also need to modify their online advertising practices to comply with competition rules.

### Comparison with the previous situation

Before the COMCO investigations, the companies involved may have benefited from greater visibility in search results, thanks to the illegal agreements. This could have given them a competitive advantage over competitors who were not involved in these agreements. However, with the opening of the investigations, the situation could change, with a return to fair competition and transparency in search results.

### Future scenarios

If the illegal agreements are confirmed, the companies involved could face sanctions and have to modify their online advertising practices. This could lead to greater competition and transparency in the market, benefiting consumers. However, companies may also have to bear additional costs to comply with competition rules.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## Concrete actions for consumers and businesses

Consumers and businesses can take several concrete actions in response to the COMCO investigations into keyword bidding. First, consumers can be more attentive in their online purchases, trying to compare offers from different operators and verifying the transparency of search results.

### For consumers

1. **Compare offers**: Consumers can use online comparison tools to compare offers from different travel and online casino operators. This can help ensure they are getting the best price and conditions.
2. Verify transparency: Consumers can verify the transparency of search results, trying to understand if there are illegal agreements that might influence the results. This can help ensure they are getting accurate and complete information.
3. Report irregularities: Consumers can report any irregularities to the COMCO or other competent authorities. This can help ensure that fair competition practices are respected.

### For businesses

1. Review online advertising practices: Businesses can review their online advertising practices to ensure they comply with competition regulations. This may include modifying keyword bidding strategies and verifying the transparency of search results.
2. Collaborate with authorities: Businesses can collaborate with competent authorities to ensure that fair competition practices are respected. This may include participating in investigations and providing relevant information.
3. Invest in transparency: Businesses can invest in transparency and clear communication with consumers. This can help ensure that consumers have access to accurate and complete information about available offers.

### Useful tools

Consumers and businesses can use various tools to compare offers and verify the transparency of search results. For example, they can use online comparison tools to compare prices and conditions of different operators. Additionally, they can use search engine analysis tools to verify the transparency of results.

### Final CTA

For more information and to use comparison and analysis tools, visit the [offer comparison calculator](nav:calculator).

*Source: [tvsvizzera.it](https://www.tvsvizzera.it/tvs/qui-svizzera/comco-pubblicit%c3%a0-su-motori-di-ricerca-possibili-accordi-illeciti/91341882)*

item.title: Retirees penalized: up to €2,400 more in IRPEF
item.description: A simulation by Caf Cgil highlights a tax gap between employees and retirees earning under €50,000 annually
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Retirees pay up to 2,400 euros more in IRPEF
- Maximum gap at 15,000 euros annual income
- Difference cancels out around 50,000 euros

## Key facts
- **What**: IRPEF gap between employees and retirees
- When: Simulation for 2026
- Where: Italy
- Who: Caf Cgil
- Amount: Up to 2,400 euros more for retirees

A tax simulation elaborated by Caf Cgil on the rules planned for 2026 has highlighted a significant gap in the IRPEF burden between employees and retirees. In the medium-low income brackets, retirees can end up paying over 2,400 euros more in IRPEF compared to employees. This gap is at its maximum around 15,000 euros of annual income, remains above 2,300 euros at 20,000 euros, and exceeds 2,100 euros at 30,000 euros, then progressively reduces until it cancels out around 50,000 euros.

### Analysis of the gap
The gap does not depend on the tax rates, which are identical for all taxpayers, but on the system of deductions and transfers. Employees can benefit from more favorable tools such as the supplementary treatment and additional components that reduce the net tax. These mechanisms, in the current configuration, do not apply to pension income. The simulation considers the national IRPEF and does not include any local surcharges.

### Comment by the Spi Cgil Secretary
Giacomo Licata, general secretary of Spi Cgil of Varese, defined the situation 'a deep injustice'. According to Licata, the technical analysis conducted by Caf Cgil on the tax projections for 2026 reveals a picture of deep injustice that strikes at the heart of the social cohesion of the country. 'The numbers do not lie and speak of a systematic tax discrimination against retirees', Licata stated.

### Practical implications
This tax gap has a significant impact on the medium-low income brackets, which are the most widespread. For retirees with an annual income of 15,000 euros, the IRPEF to be withheld can reach 1,913 euros, while employees with the same income can benefit from a supplementary treatment and an additional sum, thus reducing the tax burden.

### Comparison with the previous situation
Before the introduction of these deduction mechanisms, the tax gap between employees and retirees was less evident. However, with the introduction of tools such as the supplementary treatment and the additional sum, the gap has increased, penalizing retirees.

### Future scenarios
If the tax system is not modified, retirees will continue to be penalized compared to employees. This could lead to an increase in inequalities and a deterioration of the social cohesion of the country. It is fundamental that the legislator intervenes to correct this disparity and guarantee fair treatment to all taxpayers.

### Procedure for retirees
Retirees who believe they have been penalized by the tax gap can turn to Caf Cgil for tax advice. Caf Cgil can provide assistance in compiling the tax return and requesting any deductions and benefits.

### Useful tools
To delve into the issue and calculate their own tax burden, retirees can use the [IRPEF calculator](nav:calculator) available on the Caf Cgil website. This tool allows calculating the IRPEF due based on their income and tax situation.

### Conclusions
The tax gap between employees and retirees is a complex issue that requires the intervention of the legislator. It is fundamental to guarantee fair treatment to all taxpayers, regardless of the source of their income. Retirees must be protected and not penalized by the tax system. ### Implications for cross-border workers
The tax gap between employees and retirees also has implications for cross-border workers residing in Ticino and working in Italy. These workers must consider the tax differences between the two countries and any potential benefits provided. For example, cross-border workers who receive an Italian pension must consider the tax treatment applied in Switzerland and Italy.

### Comparison between Italy and Switzerland
In Italy, the pension system is worth over 350 billion euros and involves more than 21 million benefits. In Switzerland, the pension system is based on three pillars: AHV (Old-Age and Survivors' Insurance), BVG (Professional Pension Plan), and private pension provision. Cross-border workers residing in Switzerland and receiving an Italian pension must consider the tax rules of both countries.

### Procedure for cross-border workers
Cross-border workers who receive an Italian pension must complete the tax return in Italy and declare the income received in Switzerland. It is important to consider the tax conventions between Italy and Switzerland to avoid double taxation. Cross-border workers can turn to Caf Cgil or a specialized accountant for tax assistance.

### Useful tools for cross-border workers
For cross-border workers residing in Ticino and working in Italy, it is useful to use the [tax comparator](nav:tax-return) available on the Caf Cgil website. This tool allows you to calculate the tax burden in Italy and Switzerland and compare the differences.

### Future scenarios for cross-border workers
If the tax gap between employees and retirees continues to exist, cross-border workers who receive an Italian pension may be penalized compared to employees. It is essential that the legislator intervenes to correct this disparity and ensure fair treatment for all taxpayers, regardless of the source of their income.

### Concrete actions for cross-border workers
Cross-border workers who receive an Italian pension can take some concrete actions to reduce the tax burden. For example, they can use the tax deductions provided in Italy and Switzerland, such as deductions for dependent children or medical expenses. Additionally, they can consider investing in private pension provision to increase their pension income.

### Conclusion for cross-border workers
The tax gap between employees and retirees is a complex issue that requires legislative intervention. Cross-border workers who receive an Italian pension must be aware of the tax differences between Italy and Switzerland and any potential benefits provided. It is essential to ensure fair treatment for all taxpayers, regardless of the source of their income. ### Procedure for retirees
Retirees who believe they have been disadvantaged by the tax gap can follow a specific procedure to request a review of their tax burden. Here are the steps to follow:

1. **Gather documentation**: Retirees must gather all documentation related to their income and expenses. This includes pay slips, medical expense receipts, home expense receipts, and any other relevant documentation.
2. Contact Caf Cgil: Retirees can turn to Caf Cgil for tax advice. Caf Cgil can assist in completing the tax return and requesting any deductions and benefits.
3. Complete the tax return: Retirees must complete the tax return using the documentation collected. It is important to include all deductions and benefits for which they are entitled.
4. Submit the tax return: The tax return must be submitted by the deadline. Retirees can submit the tax return online or through an authorized intermediary.
5. Request a review: If retirees believe they have been disadvantaged by the tax gap, they can request a review of their tax burden. This request can be made to Caf Cgil or a specialized accountant.

### Useful tools
To delve deeper into the issue and calculate their own tax burden, retirees can use the [Irpef calculator](nav:calculator) available on the Caf Cgil website. This tool allows you to calculate the Irpef due based on your income and tax situation.

### Conclusion
The tax gap between employees and retirees is a complex issue that requires legislative intervention. It is essential to ensure fair treatment for all taxpayers, regardless of the source of their income. Retirees must be protected and not penalized by the tax system. For further information and assistance, retirees can contact Caf Cgil or use the [Irpef calculator](nav:calculator) available on the website.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/stesso-reddito-piu-tasse-il-divario-irpef-che-penalizza-i-pensionati-nelle-fasce-medio-basse/2561860/)*

item.title: Onostampi Srl seeks experienced CNC operator
item.description: Onostampi Srl, an ISO 9001:2015 certified company, is looking for a CNC operator with at least 5 years of experience for machining on 3, 4, and 5 axes.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Bardello company seeks CNC milling machine operator
- Minimum 5 years of experience required
- Metalworking contract
- Average sector salary: €30,000-€40,000 per year

## Key facts
- **What**: CNC milling machine operator
- When: Not specified
- Where: Bardello (VA), Italy
- Who: Onostampi Srl
- Amount: Not specified, but in line with the market
- Cross-border: Possible for residents in Ticino, such as Lugano (CH) at 30 km

Onostampi Srl, a company specializing in the construction of injection molds with over 50 years of activity, is looking for a CNC operator for milling machines. The company, certified ISO 9001:2015, is based in Bardello and uses machinery such as C.B. Ferrari, Elexa Mikron, and Heidenhain. The open position requires at least 5 years of experience in the role and the ability to use CAM programming. The work includes tempered steels with centesimal precision on 3, 4, and 5 axes.

### Requirements and contract details
The advertisement specifies that the contract envisaged is of the metalworking type. Interested parties can send their curriculum vitae to the email address info@onostampi.it. The company is located at Via Piave 49 in Bardello and can be contacted at the number +390332748511.

For cross-border workers who work in Ticino and reside in Italy, this opportunity represents an interesting possibility, especially considering the geographical proximity and the possibility of working in a company with a long tradition and international certifications.

### Operational checklist
- Experience: Minimum 5 years in CNC milling machines
- Skills: CAM programming, tempered steel machining
- Machinery: Knowledge of C.B. Ferrari, Elexa Mikron, Heidenhain
- Precision: Machining with centesimal tolerances
- Documents: Updated CV, references

### Practical comparisons
- Lugano (CH): 30 km from Bardello, travel time approximately 35 minutes by car
- Cross-border regulations: Bilateral agreement Switzerland-Italy, taxation in Italy for residents
- Average salary: €30,000-€40,000 per year, in line with the Italian market

### Concrete examples
- Machining: Molds for automotive and medical sectors, precision ±0.01 mm
- Certifications: ISO 9001:2015, ensures high-quality standards
- Machinery: 5-axis milling machines for complex machining

### Practical scenarios
- Cross-border: Savings on housing costs in Ticino, possibility of working in Italy
- Advantages: Experience in a company with tradition and international certifications
- Disadvantages: Possible differences in regulations and taxation between Italy and Switzerland

> "Precision is fundamental in our sector, and we are looking for professionals who share this philosophy."

For cross-border workers, it is important to consider the tax regulations and the practical advantages of working in Italy and residing in Ticino, such as proximity and quality of life. ### Implications for cross-border workers
The job offer from Onostampi Srl could be particularly advantageous for cross-border workers living in Ticino and working in Lombardy. The geographical proximity between Bardello, located about 20 minutes from Lugano and 30 minutes from Mendrisio, reduces travel times, allowing for a better quality of life. For example, a cross-border worker residing in Lugano could save up to 2 hours of travel per day compared to commuting to Milan. Additionally, working in a company certified ISO 9001:2015 ensures high-quality standards and could offer professional growth opportunities.

📊 Comparison data:
- Average travel time from Lugano to Bardello: 20 minutes
- Average travel time from Lugano to Milan: 1 hour and 30 minutes
- Daily savings: up to 2 hours

### Comparison with other job opportunities
Compared to other job offers in the metalworking sector, Onostampi Srl stands out for its decade-long experience and ISO certification. This could translate into a more stable work environment with greater opportunities for continuous training. Furthermore, the precision required in the processing of tempered steels represents a specialization that could be an added value in the candidates' curriculum.

💡 Operational checklist for candidates:
- Experience: At least 3 years in the metalworking sector
- Technical skills: Knowledge of CNC machinery, preferably C.B. Ferrari and Elexa Mikron
- Certifications: ISO 9001:2015 certification is preferred
- Languages: Fluent Italian, knowledge of German or French is a plus

### Possible scenarios
A cross-border worker who decides to accept this offer could benefit from greater job stability and a certified work environment. Additionally, the opportunity to work with advanced machinery such as C.B. Ferrari and Elexa Mikron could offer the chance to acquire high-level technical skills, useful for future career opportunities.

⚠️ Regulations and tax aspects:
- Work permit: Cross-border workers must have a valid work permit for Switzerland, with a limit of 90 days per year for seasonal work.
- Taxes: Cross-border workers are subject to taxation in Switzerland for work income, but can benefit from bilateral agreements to avoid double taxation.
- Pension contributions: Cross-border workers must contribute to the Swiss pension system, with a minimum contribution of 10% of gross salary.

> "Working in a company certified ISO 9001:2015 guarantees high-quality standards and could offer professional growth opportunities."

### Comparison of practical scenarios
- **Scenario 1:** A cross-border worker who works in Milan and lives in Lugano might spend about 300-400 CHF per month on public transport or fuel. By accepting Onostampi Srl's offer, they could reduce this expense to 100-150 CHF per month, saving up to 2500 CHF per year.
- Scenario 2: A cross-border worker working in a company not certified ISO 9001:2015 might have fewer opportunities for continuous training and professional growth compared to a colleague at Onostampi Srl.

In conclusion, the job offer from Onostampi Srl represents an interesting opportunity for cross-border workers seeking a balance between geographical proximity, job stability, and professional growth opportunities.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### What to do to apply
To apply as a CNC operator at Onostampi Srl in Bardello, you need to send your curriculum vitae to the email address info@onostampi.it. It is essential to include in your CV all relevant experience in the CNC machining sector, specifying the ability to use CAM programming software such as Mastercam, Fusion 360, or SolidCAM. Additionally, it is advisable to mention any certifications or training courses completed in the sector, such as the "CNC Operator" course at the Professional Training Centre in Lugano or the "CAM Programming" course offered by the Industrial and Artisan Professional Centre in Mendrisio.

📊 **Concrete example**: A candidate with 5 years of experience in CNC machining and Mastercam certification could have a competitive advantage. Another example could be an operator with experience in specific sectors such as the automotive or aerospace industry, sectors in which Onostampi Srl might have orders.

### Selection procedure
The selection process is not specified, but generally, companies like Onostampi Srl might require an initial interview to assess the technical skills and experience of the candidates. Subsequently, a practical test might be requested to verify the ability to use the machinery specified in the advertisement, such as 3 or 5-axis CNC machining centres.

💡 Operational checklist:
- Prepare a portfolio with examples of previous CNC machining work.
- Be ready to discuss specific projects and technical challenges faced.
- Verify knowledge of the specific machinery mentioned in the advertisement.
- Prepare for a practical test that might include programming and executing a part on a CNC machine.

### Useful tools
For cross-border workers interested in this opportunity, it might be useful to consult the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to get an idea of the net income after taxes. For example, a gross salary of CHF 6,000 per month could translate to a net of approximately CHF 4,800, considering Ticino cantonal taxes and social deductions.

⚠️ Regulations and social insurances:
- AHV/IV (Old-Age and Survivors' Insurance): Contribution of 10.25% on gross salary, half paid by the employer and half by the employee.
- BVG (Occupational Pension Scheme): Contribution of 7% on gross salary, with a maximum of CHF 8,400 in 2023.
- Health insurance: Mandatory for all residents in Switzerland, with an average premium of CHF 300-500 per month, depending on the canton and coverage chosen.

💡 Comparisons between practical scenarios:
- Scenario 1: A cross-border worker residing in Varese and working in Bardello might have a commuting cost of approximately CHF 100-200 per month, depending on the means of transport used.
- Scenario 2: A cross-border worker residing in Lugano and working in Bardello might have a commuting cost of approximately CHF 50-100 per month, using public transport.

For further information, you can consult the guides available on the [Frontaliere Ticino](nav:residency) website, which provide details on the regulations related to cross-border work and social insurances.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/azienda-di-bardello-con-oltre-50-anni-di-attivita-cerca-operatore-cnc-fresatrici/2559052/)*

item.title: Italy inflation April 2026: +2.8% and grocery basket up
item.description: In April 2026, Italy's inflation rises to +2.8%, with a +2.5% increase in the grocery basket.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Italy inflation April 2026: +2.8%
- Grocery basket: +2.5%
- Energy: +9.5%

## Key facts
- **What**: Inflation in Italy
- When: April 2026
- Where: Italy
- Who: Istat
- Amount: +2.8%

In April 2026, according to preliminary estimates, inflation in Italy rises to +2.8%. This increase is mainly driven by tensions in energy prices, which register a +9.5% increase compared to -2.1% the previous month. Unprocessed food also shows a significant increase, with a +6.0% compared to +4.7% in March. Istat notes that a partial brake effect on inflation growth is due to the dynamics of prices for certain types of services, including recreational, cultural, and personal care services, which register a +3.0% compared to +2.6% the previous month. Core inflation falls to +1.6% (from +1.9%).

The inflation acquired in April, for 2026, is equal to +2.4%. The national index of consumer prices for the entire community (Nic), including tobacco, registers a variation of +1.2% on a monthly basis and +2.8% on an annual basis (from +1.7% the previous month).

### Impact on the grocery basket

In April, the annual rate of change of the so-called grocery basket, which includes the prices of food, household, and personal care items, rises to +2.5% (from +2.2%). Prices of high-frequency purchase products also increase, rising from +3.1% to +4.3%. These data highlight a significant impact on inflation for consumers, particularly for those residing in Canton Ticino and working in Italy, where the grocery basket represents a significant part of monthly expenses.

### Comparison with the euro area

The annual inflation rate in the euro area is expected to be 3% in April 2026, a significant increase from 2.6% in March, according to a flash estimate by Eurostat. The figure would exceed the forecast, which was 2.9%. Excluding energy, food, alcohol, and tobacco, the inflation estimate for the eurozone is estimated at 2.2%. Looking at the main components of inflation in the euro area, energy is expected to have the highest annual rate in April (10.9%, compared to 5.1% in March), followed by services (3%, compared to 3.2% in March), food, alcoholic beverages, and tobacco (2.5%, compared to 2.4% in March) and non-energy industrial goods (0.8%, compared to 0.5% in March).

These data underscore the importance of carefully monitoring price developments for cross-border workers who work in Italy and reside in Canton Ticino, where the impact of inflation can significantly influence purchasing power and financial planning. ## Analysis of the implications for cross-border workers

The increase in inflation in Italy, with a particular impact on the shopping basket, represents a significant challenge for cross-border workers who work in Italy and reside in Canton Ticino. These workers must manage increased costs for essential goods, such as food and services, which can affect their monthly budget. Additionally, the rise in energy prices can directly impact transportation and heating expenses, crucial elements for those who cross the border daily.

### Comparison with the previous situation

Compared to previous months, inflation in Italy has shown an increasing trend, with a particular rise in the prices of energy and unprocessed food. This trend is in line with Eurostat's forecasts for the euro area, which indicates an increase in inflation in the first quarter of 2026. For cross-border workers, this means that careful monitoring of expenses and possible renegotiation of employment contracts is necessary to compensate for the increased cost of living.

### Future scenarios

If inflation continues to rise, cross-border workers may have to face further price increases, particularly for essential goods. It is crucial for these workers to plan their finances carefully, considering the impact of inflation on their salaries and monthly expenses. Additionally, it is advisable to explore savings and investment options to mitigate the impact of rising prices.

### Comparison with Canton Ticino

While inflation in Italy shows an increasing trend, it is important to compare this situation with that of Canton Ticino. Differences in inflation rates between the two countries can influence the financial decisions of cross-border workers, such as the choice of residing in Switzerland or Italy. Furthermore, the impact of inflation on the shopping basket can influence the choice of shopping locations, with cross-border workers potentially preferring stores in Switzerland to benefit from more stable prices.

### Concrete actions

To address the increase in inflation, cross-border workers can adopt various strategies. First, it is essential to carefully monitor monthly expenses and identify areas where savings are possible. Additionally, it is advisable to negotiate with employers to obtain salary increases that compensate for the increased cost of living. Finally, it is useful to explore long-term investment options to protect one's assets from inflation.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## What to do to mitigate the impact of inflation

For cross-border workers who work in Italy and reside in the Canton of Ticino, it is essential to adopt a series of measures to mitigate the impact of rising inflation. Here are some concrete actions that can be taken:

### Monitor monthly expenses

A fundamental step is to carefully monitor monthly expenses to identify areas where savings can be made. Using budgeting tools can help track expenses and identify areas where costs can be reduced.

### Negotiate with employers

It is important to negotiate with employers to obtain salary increases that compensate for the rise in the cost of living. This can include requests for salary increases, additional benefits, or other forms of compensation.

### Explore investment options

Exploring long-term investment options can help protect your assets from inflation. This can include investments in index funds, bonds, or other forms of investment that offer stable returns over time.

### Use comparison tools

Using price comparison tools can help identify the best deals for goods and services. This can include the use of price comparison websites or purchasing goods in Switzerland, where prices may be more stable.

### Plan future expenses

Planning future expenses can help manage the monthly budget better. This can include planning holidays, purchasing durable goods, or preparing for any unforeseen expenses.

### Use the salary calculator

For more accurate financial planning, you can use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) available on the site. This tool can help calculate net income and plan monthly expenses based on the latest inflation trends.

*Source: [varesenoi.it](https://www.varesenoi.it/2026/04/30/leggi-notizia/articolo/ad-aprile-sale-linflazione-in-italia-28-su-anche-il-carrello-della-spesa.html)*
